author: Dr. Phong Le, MD
department: Primary Care — Charm City Asian Health Center
content:
ANNUAL PRIMARY CARE VISIT — 11/8/2022, 11:00 AM
Patient: Nguyen, Lan Thi (Age 68)
Insurance: Medicare A+B + Maryland Medicaid (dual eligible since 2019). WellCare Part D.
Vietnamese-language visit.

INTERVAL HISTORY (since 2021 annual):
Ms. Nguyen reports she has been doing reasonably well in retirement. Major events:
- Older brother (the heavy smoker, age 64) was DIAGNOSED WITH ADVANCED LUNG CANCER in Hanoi over the summer (squamous cell, stage IV). Family devastated. She has been spending more time on phone calls with him; he is receiving palliative care in Vietnam, prognosis poor.
- She has been more anxious about her own health since hearing the diagnosis. Specifically worried about her lungs given her own (former) smoking history. Wants to discuss what screening is appropriate.
- Right TKA from 2018 is doing well; minimal pain; full ROM ~115°; has not needed any analgesics for that knee in a year.
- Left knee, however, has begun to bother her more — she anticipates eventually needing it replaced too.
- Tai chi 2x/week back to normal frequency since husband's death (2018).
- Lives with son Quang (47), daughter-in-law Linh (44), and grandchildren (now 11 and 7).

No new chest, cardiac, or GI symptoms. Specifically denies cough, hemoptysis, dyspnea, chest pain, weight loss. No fever. No new joint complaints other than the left knee.

ROS otherwise negative.

PMH:
- Hypertension (well-controlled on lisinopril)
- Hyperlipidemia (well-controlled on atorvastatin, LDL ~80)
- s/p Right TKA (4/2018, Dr. Reilly at Hopkins Bayview — excellent outcome)
- Latent TB infection (positive PPD/IGRA 2010, untreated — asymptomatic, declined INH historically; recently re-discussed in 2020 given COVID risk concerns; she again declined)
- Left knee osteoarthritis (KL grade 2, conservative)
- Post-menopausal

MEDS (Medicare-Medicaid pharmacy reconciled):
- Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily
- Atorvastatin 40 mg PO daily
- Aspirin 81 mg PO daily (started at age 65 for primary prevention; will need to re-discuss given current 2022 USPSTF guidance against starting in older adults)
- Calcium 600 mg PO daily + vitamin D3 1000 IU daily (added 2020 after low DEXA)
- Multivitamin daily
- Acetaminophen 500–1000 mg PO PRN
- Ibuprofen 400–600 mg PO PRN — uses 2–3x/week for left knee

FAMILY HISTORY UPDATE: As above, brother just diagnosed with stage IV squamous cell lung cancer.

VITALS: BP 134/78, HR 72, RR 14, T 98.0, SpO2 98% RA. Weight 62.4 kg, BMI 26.3.

EXAM:
General: Pleasant, in good spirits but visibly emotional when discussing brother.
HEENT: Normal.
CV: RRR, no murmur. No peripheral edema. Pulses normal.
Lungs: CTAB.
Abdomen: Soft, NT/ND. No organomegaly. No bruits.
MSK: Right knee — well-healed surgical scar, no effusion, full ROM, no tenderness. Left knee — mild medial tenderness, no effusion, full ROM with crepitus.
Neuro / Psych: Intact; mood mildly low but appropriate to circumstances.

LABS (drawn today, results back):
- BMP: Na 138, K 4.4, Cl 102, CO2 26, BUN 22 (slight up), Cr 1.2 (eGFR 47 — has declined from 0.9-1.0 over the years; suggests CKD III). Glu 102.
- LFTs: AST 24, ALT 22, ALP 70, T-bili 0.6, Alb 4.0 (all normal).
- LIPID PANEL: TC 168, LDL 82 (at goal), HDL 50, TG 145.
- HbA1c 5.6 (pre-diabetic range — repeat next year).
- TSH 2.85 (normal).
- CBC: WBC 6.1, Hgb 11.8 (mildly low; new from previously 12+; will recheck), Plt 268.
- UA: 1+ protein (new), no blood, no leuks/nitrites. Spot urine ACR pending.

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
1. HTN — well-controlled. Continue lisinopril 10 mg.
2. HLD — well-controlled at goal LDL. Continue atorvastatin 40 mg.
3. NEW: CHRONIC KIDNEY DISEASE, likely stage 2/3a (eGFR 47, Cr 1.2 — has been trending up over 12 years from baseline 0.9, with new 1+ proteinuria). Counseled on this. Likely contributors: long-standing HTN, chronic NSAID use (concerning), and aging. Plan:
   → Strongly counseled patient to MINIMIZE ibuprofen use given her CKD. Continue acetaminophen PRN as first line. Provided written Vietnamese instructions.
   → Spot urine ACR ordered today.
   → Recheck BMP + ACR in 8 weeks.
   → Will refer to nephrology if eGFR <45 or significant proteinuria progresses.
4. LATENT TB — reviewed again. Patient declined treatment for the 4th time. Documented.
5. LEFT KNEE OA — conservative management; continue acetaminophen first-line. Discussed AVOIDING chronic NSAIDs given new CKD.
6. LUNG CANCER SCREENING — patient is asking. We discussed: she is 68 with 14 lifetime pack-years and quit 18 years ago. The 2021 USPSTF guideline recommends annual low-dose chest CT for adults 50–80 with ≥20 pack-years who currently smoke or quit within 15 years. She does NOT formally meet criteria (14 pack-years < 20; quit > 15 years ago). However, given her family history of advanced lung cancer in her brother, I discussed shared decision-making about a one-time low-dose chest CT. She was interested but expressed concern about cost (Medicare covers LDCT screening only for those meeting USPSTF criteria; she would have to pay out-of-pocket; estimated $250 at Hopkins).
   → SHARED DECISION: she would like to think about it and discuss with her son. Will revisit next visit.
7. ASPIRIN 81 mg — reviewed against 2022 USPSTF guidance recommending against initiating in adults >60 for primary prevention. Patient is 68, no known CAD, no prior MI/CVA. We discussed stopping vs continuing. She prefers to continue for now since she has tolerated it. Acknowledged this is somewhat against current guidance; her risk tolerance favors continuation.
8. BREAST CANCER SCREENING — mammogram 6/2022, BIRADS 1, negative.
9. COLON CANCER SCREENING — colonoscopy 4/2019 with 1 small tubular adenoma, low-risk pathology, rescreen scheduled 2029.
10. DEXA — bone density 2020 osteopenia (T-score -1.8 femoral neck). Calcium + vit D maintained.
11. VACCINATIONS — flu today; PCV13 + PPSV23 both up to date; Shingrix x 2 done. Tdap 2018.
12. Annual physical in 12 months.

Follow-up: 8 weeks for repeat BMP + ACR + Hgb. Next annual 11/2023.

Visit time: 60 minutes.
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Phong Le, MD
Charm City Asian Health Center
Date: 11/8/2022
